items.title: ETS back on track after power outage, but eight services running late
items.summary: However, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) in a statement today said that several ETS services on the KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral route are still experiencing temporary delays to enable the operation schedule to be stabilised. KTMB said as of 3.30 pm today, eight ETS services are still experiencing delays, namely train EX9573 which is three hours and 34 minutes late, EP9524 (two hours and 35 minutes), EP9326 (one hour and 40 minutes), EP9428 (25 minutes). Also experiencing delays are trains EP9323 (two hours and 11 minutes), EG9343 (one hour and 14 minutes), EG9442 (two hours and 51 minutes) and EX9575 (one hour).

items.title: Family reunited after 95 years across Malaysia–Indonesia border
items.summary: Family member Rosmi Jamat, 64, a retired teacher from Bandar Jengka, said her late father, Jamat Jaikaran, often advised his nine children to try to find relatives left behind in Pacitan, East Java. "My late father boarded a ship and stopped in Singapore before continuing his journey to Port Dickson to start a new life in Linggi, Malaya," she said. Rosmi said after obtaining phone numbers and contacting relatives in Indonesia, the information shared matched her late father's account.

items.title: Foreigners caught running illegal transport services with rented cars in Terengganu - RTD
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has detected the activities of foreigners in the state who use rented vehicles to carry passengers and transport goods. Terengganu RTD director Mohd Zamri Samion said investigations found that most of the individuals detained involved Rohingya ethnic groups who offered the service using private vehicles rented from locals. "We have detected the activities of foreigners, especially Rohingya, who use private vehicles rented by locals to carry passengers, transport children and carry various tasks in their daily activities.

items.title: Switzerland votes on proposal to cap population at 10mil
items.summary: ZURICH: Switzerland voted on Sunday on whether to back a proposal to cap the country's population in a referendum likened to Britain's Brexit vote, which could have far-reaching consequences for the economy and Bern's relations with the European Union. Driven by concern about immigration, pressure on public services and housing, the constitutional change pitched by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, or SVP, would mandate that the population must not exceed 10 million by 2050. Switzerland's ageing population is already above 9 million and polls suggest public opinion is finely balanced.

items.title: Bersama targets 50pct women, 70pct under-50 candidates, Rafizi says
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) is targeting 50 per cent women candidates and 70 per cent candidates aged 50 and below in any election it contests, including the upcoming Negri Sembilan election. President Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said the party's decision to contest independently, without joining any coalition, had made the candidate selection process more straightforward. We just need qualified women candidates from the Malay, Chinese and Indian communities to come forward and become our candidates," he said at the Jelajah Kancil Negri Sembilan event last night.

items.title: Australia tribunal rejects move by Japan's Inpex to stop LNG strike
items.summary: PERTH: An Australian labour tribunal on Sunday rejected an application by Japanese gas company Inpex to halt a strike by some 400 oil and gas workers at its Ichthys LNG project. The Fair Work Commission (FWC) rejected Inpex's claim that a shutdown would hurt the Australian economy due to lost export revenue and would risk dangerous blackouts. Bill Townsend, Inpex's corporate senior vice president, called the result "disappointing" and confirmed an updated offer for employees was being prepared.

items.title: Police probe theft of laptop bag from Setapak kiosk, suspect sought
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating the theft of a laptop bag containing cash, personal belongings and a laptop from a UniFi kiosk at Setapak Sentral in Setapak. Wangsa Maju police chief Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman said a police report was lodged on June 11 by the victim, who said the bag had been left on a chair at his workplace. The stolen bag contained personal items, cash and a laptop, he said in a statement today.

items.title: Maybe just stay home tonight: Storms sweeping Malaysia until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning forecasting thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds across almost all states and Kuala Lumpur until 9pm today. MetMalaysia, in a warning issued at 4.35pm, said adverse weather conditions are expected in Perlis, Penang, Perak and Kuala Lumpur. Other states expected to be affected are Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu), Kelantan (Gua Musang), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin).

items.title: Two unrelated missing persons found drowned in Sungai Kedah, 100m apart
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: Two persons, including a teenager with a learning disability who had been reported missing since last Friday, were found drowned in Sungai Kedah here today. Kota Setar police chief ACP Syed Basri Syed Ali said the body of Saw Chee Wan, 16, was identified by his family members, who had earlier reported him missing from his home in Kampung Seberang Perak near here, located about two metres from the scene. "The body was found lodged against a tree near Taman Pesisiran Jeragan, Ampang Jajar, by a cleaning worker from the Alor Setar City Council (MBAS) at 9am today," he told a press conference at the scene.

items.title: 5 arrested over robberies and assaults via fake dating profiles in Johor
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Police have arrested five men linked to a robbery syndicate targeting victims met through a gay dating application. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the suspects, aged 21 to 28, were detained at 11.30am in Brinchang, Cameron Highlands, Pahang, by a team from the Johor Baru Selatan district police headquarters (IPD). Ab Rahaman said police also seized five mobile phones, a Perodua Bezza, a gold purchase receipt, five debit cards, four necklaces, four pairs of earrings, two rings and two gold bracelets.

items.title: Sabah doesn’t just need films, it needs a film culture, says award-winning filmmaker Nadira Ilana amid industry brain drain
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Award-winning Malaysian filmmaker Nadira Ilana (pic) said Sabah’s creative industry was deliberately set back by the National Cultural Policy of 1971, stripping Sabahans of the cultural and linguistic freedoms needed to build a thriving arts sector. “The creative industries in Sabah was ‘sabotaged’. Sabahans were making our own documentaries in the 1970s,” she said at the Daily Express Creative Minds Forum 2026, to spotlight and celebrate the voices shaping Sabah’s creative landscape.

items.title: MQA: Malaysian higher education meets international standards
items.summary: SINTOK: The quality of Malaysia's higher education remains internationally recognised and should not be linked to concerns over graduate employability. Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA) chief executive officer Professor Datuk Dr Mohammad Shatar Sabran said graduates today need to equip themselves with added value and a broader range of skills. He said UUM regularly seeks feedback from industry players through its industry advisory council and is planning to establish an international industry advisory panel comprising representatives from multinational companies to ensure its programmes remain relevant.

items.title: HLIB turns bullish on Oriental Kopi, lifts call to 'Buy'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Bank Bhd (HLIB) said shares of Oriental Kopi Holdings Bhd may offer a more attractive risk-reward profile following a sharp correction, with the bank seeing upside potential as the stock stabilises near key support levels. "That said, price action is deeply oversold and stabilising around 89 sen, which aligns with the DT measured target. HLIB said a sustained move above RM1.00 could trigger upside towards RM1.06 and RM1.20, while downside risks remain towards 86 sen and 82 sen should the stock breach support.

items.title: Authorities arrest 353 people in forest reserve encroachment crackdown
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Authorities have arrested 353 people, including Indonesians, Myanmar nationals, Bangladeshis and Cambodians, for offences involving forest reserve encroachment since last year, with Kelantan recording the most cases. Peninsular Malaysia Forestry Department director-general Datuk Zahari Ibrahim said 202 people were detained in 2025, while another 151 were arrested between January and March this year. Zahari said the department recorded 162 cases of permanent forest reserve encroachment nationwide in 2025 and another 45 cases between January and March this year.

items.title: Where’s that umbrella: Thunderstorms, heavy rainfall expected all over Malaysia till up to 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in almost all states apart from Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya until 6pm today. MetMalaysia in its warning at 2.25 pm said the weather conditions are expected to occur throughout the states of Perak, Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya. In Kedah, the areas affected are Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu, while in Penang it includes Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan.

items.title: HLIB keeps 'Buy' on IJM, sees upside after 13.7pct share price rebound
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Investment Bank (HLIB) has kept its "Buy" call on IJM Corp Bhd, with a target price of RM3.02 that is based on a 20 per cent discount to its sum-of-parts (SOP) valuation of RM3.77. The bank said IJM's recent share price correction had brought valuations to more attractive levels, with the stock trading at 16.6 times and 16.8 times projected price-to-earnings ratios for financial years 2027 and 2028 (FY27/FY28), respectively. HLIB expects operational momentum to strengthen across IJM's key divisions, supported by a solid construction order book and improving property sales.

items.title: CIMB maintains 'Buy' on QL Resources, sees re-rating potential on earnings recovery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd has maintained a "Buy" call on QL Resources Bhd with a target price of RM4.60. "We believe value has emerged post the 24.2 per cent share price retracement over the past 12 months, as QL Resources returns to an earnings growth trajectory. "We see scope for QL Resources to re-rate closer to its five-year mean, backed by its diversified earnings base, defensive demand profile, and strong execution track record," it added.

items.title: ‘Good luck’: Khaled responds to former Umno figures joining PAS
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin responded with a terse “good luck” when asked about the decision by several former party figures to join PAS. Speaking after a working visit to the 4th Royal Artillery Regiment at Segamat Camp today, Khaled, who is also defence minister, said former minister Datuk Mohd Zaid Ibrahim had left Umno long ago and that his decision to join PAS was not unexpected. Among those introduced were Zaid; former Negeri Sembilan executive councillor and former Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Mohamad Rafie Abdul Malek; Rahimi Osman, a former private secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim; and former Perak executive councillor and former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman.

items.title: Warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain in almost entire country up to 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in almost all states apart from Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya until 6pm today. MetMalaysia in its warning at 2.25pm said the weather conditions are expected to occur throughout the states of Perak, Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya. In Kedah, the areas affected are Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu, while in Penang it includes Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan.

items.title: PTPTN sets aside RM3bil to help students pursue higher education
items.summary: TASEK GELUGOR: The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated about RM3 billion this year to support students pursuing higher education at institutions of higher learning nationwide. Each year, we allocate about RM3 billion to assist both new and existing students. Norliza said PTPTN also provides an advance loan payment (WPP) of RM1,500 to help new students meet initial expenses before registering at their institutions.

items.title: MetMalaysia steps up preparations for El Nino-influenced hot, dry weather
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) is intensifying the monitoring of the El Nino phenomenon that is expected to begin influencing weather patterns soon, till early next year. Hisham said the department has stepped up preparedness measures by strengthening the dissemination of weather information and early warnings to government agencies, stakeholders and the public. kw: Malaysia, news, nation, El Nino, MetMalaysia, southwest monsoon, hot weather, dry weather, myCuaca, monitoring, NST, Malaysia news © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: CIMB raises MPI target price to RM57, maintains 'Buy' on stronger AI demand outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd has reiterated its "Buy" recommendation on Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI) and raised its target price to RM57 from RM50, citing improved visibility in artificial intelligence (AI)-driven demand and firmer investor sentiment toward the technology sector. CIMB Securities said MPI is expected to benefit from its recent development of new packaging solutions for voltage power delivery (VPD)-enabled power modules targeting artificial intelligence (AI) data centre applications. CIMB Securities has raised MPI's FY28 earning per share by 9.1 per cent to reflect stronger growth in next-generation power modules and inventory replenishment in the automotive sector.

items.title: Six Madani recreational parks in Penang to be completed by year-end
items.summary: State Local Government, Town and Country Planning Committee chairman Jason H’ng Mooi Lye said three projects under the Penang Island City Council (MBPP) have commenced implementation, while another three under the Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) are in the final stage of the quotation process. “The three projects under MBPP are the upgrading of the Jalan Cheeseman neighbourhood park in Jelutong, Jalan Kampung Bukit neighbourhood park in Bayan Lepas, and Lengkok Merbah 3 neighbourhood park, also in Bayan Lepas. For Seberang Perai, he said the projects involve landscape upgrading works at open spaces in Lorong Pauh 2, Taman Pauh and Jalan Kelisa Emas 4, Taman Kelisa in Seberang Perai Tengah, as well as Lorong Belatuk 3/Pekeliling Belatuk, Taman Belatuk in Nibong Tebal, Seberang Perai Selatan.

items.title: RHB keeps 'Overweight' on banking sector, cites attractive yields despite modest earnings growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's banking sector is expected to deliver a stronger performance in the second quarter (Q2) of 2026 supported by stabilising marked-to-market (MTM) losses, improving investor sentiment and wealth traction. RHB Research said the sector had a subdued start to the year as weaker non-II plus a rise in credit cost, partly due to a base effect, dampened bottom-line growth. RHB Research believes that the banking sector in Q2 2026 appears to be a better quarter sequentially as MTM losses suffered in Q1 2026 stabilise while some banks saw improved investor sentiment and wealth traction.

items.title: PublicInvest cautious on SDS outlook despite FY27 earnings boost from Mamee plants
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) expects the factories acquired by SDS Group Bhd from Mamee Bakery Sdn Bhd to start contributing progressively to earnings in the financial year ending March 31, 2027 (FY27). It maintained its earnings forecasts for the Johor Bahru-based manufacturer and retailer of bakery products. Its indirect wholly-owned subsidiary, SDS Top Baker Sdn Bhd, signed the agreements in August 2025.

items.title: PTPTN allocates RM3b for higher education financing this year
items.summary: TASEK GELUGOR, June 14 — The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated about RM3 billion this year to support students pursuing higher education at institutions of higher learning. Its chairman Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim said the allocation, channelled through PTPTN education loans, is aimed at ensuring no student is deprived of higher education due to financial constraints. Norliza said PTPTN also provides an Advance Loan Payment (WPP) of RM1,500 to help new students meet initial expenses before registering at their institutions.

items.title: Police seek public’s help to trace missing tahfiz student in Ampang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police are seeking the public’s assistance to trace a 15-year-old boy who was reported missing after he was believed to have left a tahfiz school in Ulu Kelang, Ampang, on Friday. Ampang Jaya Police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said teachers at the school discovered that Muhammad Daniel Azizan Abdullah was missing and contacted his mother at 8.15 pm on Friday. Anyone with information on his whereabouts is urged to contact investigating officer Insp Nur Alia Dyana Raimi at 017-6515201, or the Ampang Jaya Police headquarters control centre at 03-42897222, or the nearest police station.

items.title: El Nino could bring heat, haze and water shortages into 2027, MetMalaysia warns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Malaysia is expected to experience hotter and drier conditions in the coming months as the El Niño phenomenon is forecast to begin influencing the country’s weather patterns, potentially lasting until early next year, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said today. The department warned that the conditions could reduce rainfall in several areas, increasing the risk of water shortages, forest and peatland fires, as well as haze episodes. “As a preparedness measure, MetMalaysia has strengthened the dissemination of weather information and early warnings to the government, relevant agencies and the public,” the statement said.

items.title: Transport firms' diesel subsidies now assessed based on usage pattern - Fuziah
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry is reviewing the diesel subsidy mechanism for land goods transport companies, focusing on usage-based allocations rather than a blanket increase. Its deputy minister, Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh, said the ministry is reviewing available data to ensure subsidies are distributed according to actual needs and operational performance. Meanwhile, Fuziah said she would raise a proposal to increase the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) initiative fuel subsidy allocation for registered media practitioners with the Finance Ministry.

items.title: Cases involving Najib, Akmal Saleh among several court highlights this coming week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several high-profile criminal and constitutional cases are scheduled to come before the courts this week, including proceedings involving former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak. Others include former armed forces chief General (Rtd) Tan Sri Mohd Nizam Jaffar, Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh and Muhammed Yusoff Rawther. The case attracted widespread public interest due to Yusoff's former association with Anwar.

items.title: Lost but unharmed: Four women found safe after Bukit Tunggul hike
items.summary: SEPANG: Four women who became lost while hiking at Bukit Tunggul were found safe and brought out of the area by firefighters today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the hikers had lost their way during the climb. "All four women were successfully located by firefighters and escorted to a safe area.

items.title: Produce evidence of alleged Negri Sembilan plot - Asyraf
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has rejected allegations that the party was involved in efforts to destabilise or bring down the Negri Sembilan state government, said its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. Asyraf said Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) had provided support to the state government until the expiry of its mandate, and had remained committed to ensuring administrative stability throughout that period. "Umno has never betrayed the commitments that were agreed upon," he said.

items.title: Khind expands rent-to-own push with first physical store launch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Khind Malaysia has launched its first physical rent-to-own (RTO) store in Semenyih, expanding its consumer financing model beyond digital platforms as it continues to evolve from its roots as a small electrical repair shop. Founded in 1961 in Sekinchan, Selangor, Khind began as a modest electrical repair business before gradually expanding through product innovation, distribution growth and brand development into a home solutions company. The company, operated by the Cheng family and listed on Bursa Malaysia as Khind Holdings Bhd, said the milestone reflects decades of transformation driven by continuous innovation in household appliances and customer-focused solutions.

items.title: Zahid welcomes new political party, urges unity
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has welcomed the formation of a new Malay-based political party, expressing hope that it would strengthen unity rather than divide the community. He said any individual or political figure was free to establish a new political platform, provided it contributed towards uniting Malays and advancing national interests. He added that Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative had successfully contributed to fostering national unity.

items.title: Blood, sweat and tears — and lost cash: KL police tracing mule accounts in BTS ticket scam probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in connection with alleged fraud involving the sale of tickets for BTS’ upcoming concert in Malaysia. According to Berita Harian , Brickfields district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing and several bank accounts suspected of being involved had already been identified. Hoo said no new police reports had been lodged in relation to the case so far.

items.title: BTS ticket scam: Police track suspected mule bank accounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in scams involving ticket sales for South Korean K-pop supergroup BTS's concert in December. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing, with several bank accounts identified so far. "It is possible that these bank accounts are mule accounts," he said when contacted today.

items.title: Rosmah lodges police report over US$13mil luxury mansion purchase claims [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has lodged a police report over social media allegations linking her to the purported purchase of a US$13 million luxury property for her son-in-law. The posts, said to have been circulated on X and Facebook, claimed the wife of jailed former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak was involved in financing the acquisition of the property. Rosmah denied all the allegations, describing them as baseless and unsupported by credible evidence.

items.title: RM10mil allocated for programme to support 1,300 retiring armed forces personnel
items.summary: SEGAMAT: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) has allocated RM10 million this year for the implementation of its Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme, benefiting about 1,300 Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) personnel approaching retirement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the allocation for the intervention programme forms part of the ministry's efforts to safeguard the welfare and livelihood of military veterans. He said the WBL programme enables the experience and expertise acquired by personnel throughout their service in the MAF to be formally assessed and recognised in collaboration with higher education institutions.

items.title: Zaid Ibrahim vows to reshape Pas image, says party can lead fairer Malaysia
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Former de facto law minister Datuk Zaid Ibrahim has pledged to help reshape public perception of Pas, describing the party as capable of governing a multicultural Malaysia based on justice and equality. Zaid, who officially joined Pas together with 13 other political figures ahead of the Negri Sembilan state election, said he was warmly received by the party's top leadership when he was admitted as a member last night. Also joining Pas are former Perak state executive councillor and former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman, and former Negeri Sembilan Amanah chairman Dr Rosli Yaakup, who is also a former Pas commissioner for the state.

items.title: Police identify two bodies found in Kedah River, rule out link between deaths
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 14 — Police have identified one of two men whose bodies were recovered from the Kedah River today as a 16-year-old boy who had been reported missing two days earlier, adding that there was no indication the deaths were linked. Harian Metro reported Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali as saying that the teenager, identified as Saw Chee Wan, was believed to have left his home on June 12. Although the two bodies were found about 100 metres apart, police said investigations showed the cases were unrelated.

items.title: Loke: Onn Hafiz has attended Transport Ministry functions many times before
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has hit back at Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's claims about not being invited to Transport Ministry events. The transport minister said he and his party harboured no ill will towards Onn Hafiz despite recent exchanges between the two. He said DAP would not force anyone to work with the party.

items.title: Drones more effective than boat patrols along Sungai Golok, say Kelantan police
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 14 — The use of drones is more effective than conventional boat patrols in curbing smuggling activities along the Sungai Golok border, as syndicates can easily detect patrol boats operating in the narrow river, Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said. He said the movement and engine noise of Marine Police patrol boats can be heard from across the border, allowing smugglers to suspend their activities before enforcement personnel can take action. Mohd Yusoff said 300 General Operations Force (GOF) personnel are deployed each month to monitor the 91-kilometre border stretch through 17 control posts.

items.title: Court ruling clears way for Highland Towers demolition before year end, says MPAJ
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The remaining blocks of the Highland Towers condominium in Hulu Kelang are expected to be demolished before the end of the year, following a Magistrates’ Court decision allowing the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council (MPAJ) to proceed with the long-delayed plan. MPAJ deputy president Hasrolnizam Shaari said the court approved the council’s application last week, paving the way for the demolition of the abandoned structures that have stood vacant for more than three decades. Within this year, the demolition process will take place,” he told Harian Metro today.

items.title: Selangor Bomba: Four women rescued after losing their way on Bukit Tunggul hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Four women who lost their way while hiking at Bukit Tunggul in Dengkil were found safe and escorted out by firefighters today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said authorities received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the group had become disoriented during the hike. “All four women were located by firefighters and escorted to a safe area.

items.title: Two bodies found 100m apart in Kedah River near Alor Setar bridge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The bodies of two males, aged 16 and 46, were found in the Kedah River near the Jeragan Waterfront Bridge in Alor Setar today. Kedah Fire and Rescue Department Zone 1 chief Ahmad Aminuddin Abd Rahim said firefighters were first alerted at 8.45am after receiving a report of a body caught among debris in the river. Following checks carried out with police officers, firefighters retrieved the second body from the river.

items.title: Zahid says new Malay parties must unite, not divide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said new Malay-based political parties were welcome, provided they helped unite rather than further divide the community. Speaking after the Majlis Zikir Peringatan Haul Asmaulhusna 2026 at Masjid Sultan Iskandar in Bandar Dato’ Onn, Johor Bahru, today, the Barisan Nasional chairman said any individual or political figure was free to establish a new party as long as it helped unite Malays rather than deepen existing divisions. “Whatever platform is used, if in the end we are able to unite our actions around the national agenda, the Islamic agenda and the agenda of our community, insya-Allah.

items.title: Rosmah denies role in alleged US$13m property deal, lodges police report over online claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has denied allegations linking her and her family to an alleged US$13 million (RM53 million) property transaction, saying she had lodged a police report and would pursue legal action against those responsible for disseminating the claims online. In a statement issued today in response to videos circulating on social media, the wife of former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak said she had no involvement, either directly or indirectly, in the acquisition, transfer or ownership of any luxury property, including one allegedly valued at US$13 million. Rosmah said the videos had harmed her family’s reputation.

items.title: Two married couples held over viral video of domestic helper assault, says Johor police chief
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 14 — Two married couples were arrested yesterday to assist in investigations after a video showing domestic helpers being assaulted in Taman Johor near Tampoi went viral on social media. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the couples, aged between 30 and 34, were arrested at a house in the residential area at about 7.30 pm by personnel from the Johor Criminal Investigation Department (CID) and the Johor Bahru Utara district police headquarters. Meanwhile, Ab Rahaman said police had seized mobile phones, clothing belonging to the suspects, a closed-circuit television (CCTV) camera and the two domestic helpers’ passports.

items.title: Seremban police detain man suspected of abusing stepson
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 14 — Police have arrested a man on suspicion of abusing his two-year-old stepson here yesterday. Seremban district police chief ACP Mohd Yatim Osman said police received a report on the incident from the child's biological mother at about 8.30pm before arresting the 24-year-old suspect in front of Tuanku Ja'afar Hospital (HTJ) about two hours later. Mohd Yatim said the couple had only recently married, tying the knot in April this year.

items.title: IAEA chief: Malaysia on solid footing to pursue nuclear power
items.summary: VIENNA, June 14 — Malaysia is well-positioned to pursue a nuclear power programme, having built a long-standing tradition and expertise in nuclear technology, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi said. He said Malaysia’s experience in operating research reactor has provided the country with a strong foundation to advance its nuclear energy ambition as part of its future power mix. “There are many people in Malaysia who know nuclear technology and know what a nuclear reactor is,” he added.

items.title: Anwar: Attempts to topple stable Negeri Sembilan govt amount to ‘a degree of betrayal’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said attempts to bring down the Negeri Sembilan government despite political and economic stability amounted to “a degree of betrayal”. Asked about recent claims of betrayal linked to discussions on electoral arrangements among coalition partners, Anwar, who’s also the Pakatan Harapan chairman, said such concerns would not arise if there was no betrayal. Anwar also urged political parties to focus on economic growth and issues affecting the public instead of becoming consumed by election speculation.

items.title: Five arrested after men lured through fake Grindr profiles lose RM315,000 in cash and jewellery, Johor police say
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Five men have been arrested over an alleged robbery scheme in which victims were lured through fake profiles on the dating app Grindr before being threatened and relieved of their valuables, Johor police said today. Johor police chief Comm Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the suspects, aged between 21 and 28, were believed to have targeted men by posing as potential romantic partners and arranging meetings through the social media platform. Ab Rahaman said victims were persuaded to meet the suspects for dates before being brought into a vehicle.

items.title: Dengue cases up 27pc nationwide, with Sabah among the hardest hit, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Dengue cases nationwide rose 27 per cent to 33,367 as of June 13 (Epidemiological Week 23), compared with 27,640 cases recorded during the corresponding period last year. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said the situation in Sabah was more concerning, with the state recording a sharp 50.4 per cent increase to 2,866 cases, compared with 1,905 cases during the same period last year. He said several districts in Sabah had been identified as the main contributors to the state’s caseload, namely Kota Kinabalu, Kota Marudu, Tawau, Sandakan, Penampang and Putatan.

items.title: ‘No buyers, no sellers’: Sarawak deputy minister calls for crackdown on purchasers of stolen cables
items.summary: KUCHING, June 14 — Efforts to curb vandalism and cable theft affecting public facilities must go beyond arresting perpetrators and focus on those who purchase stolen cables, said Datuk Ibrahim Baki. He said this when met after officiating a smart street lighting project at Kampung Nombor Satok here on Friday. Ibrahim pointed out that cable theft continues to affect public infrastructure and utility facilities, resulting not only in financial losses but also posing safety risks to the public.

items.title: Only RM1,000 returned after Kuching retiree ‘invests’ RM9m in alleged online IPO scam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — A 79-year-old man in Kuching has lost more than RM9 million after allegedly falling victim to a fraudulent online investment scheme involving non-existent share investments and initial public offerings (IPOs). Kuching police chief Assistant Commissioner Alexson Naga Chabu said the district police headquarters’ Commercial Crime Investigation Division had recently received a report from the victim, who suffered losses amounting to RM9.08 million. According to Alexson, the victim was persuaded to participate in the purported share investment and IPO scheme and subsequently made 27 separate transactions into nine different bank accounts provided by the suspect.

items.title: Bukit Aman warns those caught with child sexual abuse material may escalate to more serious sexual offences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police have warned that individuals who access or possess online child sexual abuse material (CSAM) may escalate to more serious offences, including grooming, sextortion and sexual crimes against children. She said repeated exposure to CSAM could lead to behavioural addiction, a condition in which individuals are compelled to seek more content and spend longer periods accessing such material. Siti Kamsiah said investigations had found that online communities sharing the material typically operated within closed groups, whose members often did not know one another personally.

items.title: Not your usual roadside hazard: Three-metre crocodile rescued from Banting drain
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said firefighters were alerted to the unusual reptile rescue at 9.07am. A team from the Banting Fire and Rescue Station rushed to the scene, where they discovered the hefty crocodile trapped in the drain near the road. The rescue operation involved eight firefighters and two fire engines.

items.title: PAS says Hamzah Zainudin’s return as Opposition Leader was always the plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — PAS said Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's return as Opposition Leader had long been its preferred outcome, adding that Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia had previously been uncomfortable with him remaining in the role following his expulsion from the party. Berita Harian reported that PAS deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the Islamist party had wanted to retain Hamzah in the role from the outset because of his performance and the support he commanded among Opposition lawmakers. “So when he formed a new party (Parti Wawasan Negara) and joined us (PAS), we thought we should continue to retain him (as Opposition Leader),” Tuan Ibrahim told reporters after a pre-election ceramah at the PAS operations centre in Seremban last night.

items.title: PKR’s Bukit Batu candidate Arthur Chiong apologises over incorrect claim on Johor allocations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — PKR’s candidate for the Bukit Batu state seat, Arthur Chiong Sen Sern, has apologised to the Johor government after making an incorrect claim during his announcement as the coalition’s candidate last night. Chiong, who is also the incumbent Bukit Batu assemblyman, said he was withdrawing remarks made during the launch of the Bukit Batu election machinery. Yesterday, Pakatan Harapan secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail announced Chiong as the coalition’s candidate to defend the Bukit Batu seat in the upcoming Johor state election.

items.title: Three-hour trek in, 15 hours out: Firefighters carry injured Fraser’s Hill hiker to safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — A woman injured during a hike to Twin Peak in Fraser’s Hill was rescued after firefighters carried her through rugged jungle terrain in an operation that stretched for 15 hours. According to New Straits Times , the 43-year-old suffered a suspected fracture to her right leg while trekking between Hobbit Cave and the Pine Tree Hill trail en route to Twin Peak, which rises 1,505 metres above sea level. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said a distress call was received at 12.15pm yesterday, prompting the deployment of six firefighters from the Raub station and a mountain guide.
